title: Pumponomics swap has zero minimum output protection
claim: The _pump function performs a Uniswap V3 exactInputSingle swap with amountOutMinimum set to 0, providing no protection against price impact or adverse slippage during the swap execution.
impact: When providing liquidity, the ETH/SUP swap (Pumponomics) can execute at any rate, including near-zero output, leading to significant token loss for the protocol. The comment claims slippage is covered by _createPosition but that's for position creation, not the swap itself.
paths: 2. Locker swaps ETH for SUP via _pump with amountOutMinimum=0
3. Adversary manipulates pool price or uses MEV

title: Permissionless stopFunding allows griefing of legitimate programs
claim: The stopFunding function has no access control modifier, allowing anyone to stop a program's funding during the early-end window.
impact: An attacker can grief legitimate programs by calling stopFunding at any time after EARLY_PROGRAM_END, causing premature termination and disrupting distributions to lockers. While the program admin can restart, this creates a persistent griefing surface.
paths: 1. Legitimate program is funded and flowing
2. Attacker calls stopFunding(programId) during early-end window

title: Instant unlock penalty distribution can be griefed via front-running
claim: The _instantUnlock function distributes penalty amounts via FLUID.distribute() which is susceptible to front-running when the distribution pool has existing units.
impact: An attacker can front-run instant unlocks by depositing small amounts to trigger pool unit changes, affecting the distribution calculation. While the actual distribution may not be loss-making, the timing and allocation can be manipulated.
paths: 3. Attacker front-runs with pool unit update

title: No event emission on provideLiquidity creates observability gap
claim: The provideLiquidity function has a FIXME comment indicating missing event emission (line 443: '/// FIXME : add event emit here').
impact: Off-chain systems and Indexer protocols cannot track liquidity provision events, creating a observability gap that hinders protocol monitoring and user experience.
paths: 2. Position is created with activePositionCount incremented
3. No event is emitted for external tracking

title: Permissionless distributeTaxAdjustment enables timing manipulation
claim: The distributeTaxAdjustment function has no access control, allowing anyone to trigger distribution of the controller's token balance.
impact: Although distribution is the intended behavior, the permissionless nature allows MEV extraction or timing manipulation. An attacker can frontrun the call when large balances accumulate to capture favorable distribution timing, potentially extracting value through pool composition.
paths: 1. Controller accumulates token balance over time
3. Attacker calls distributeTaxAdjustment to trigger distribution at favorable moment

title: Potential division truncation in flow rate calculations
claim: The startFunding function calculates flow rates via integer division: subsidyAmount / programDuration and fundingAmount / programDuration.
impact: Integer truncation may result in slightly underflowing flow rates when totalAmount is not evenly divisible by duration, causing minor dust amount accumulation in the contract over time.
paths: 1. Owner calls startFunding with non-divisible amount/duration
